feedback.text: Greetings! First, I'd like to say that to me it looks like the Digital Fairness Act is covering some issues that needed to be addressed for a while now. So thank you for putting this forward. In addition, I would like to suggest another point added to the act - one covering ownership of digital media, specifically video games. As of now, there are many video games that require online connectivity to function which means that at any point the publishers and/or developers can decide to shut down the servers which would lead to that video game being no longer accessible to customers who paid money for it. It has happened to hundreds of video games in the past already, it's happening currently and if nothing is done about it will keep happening in future as well. At the end of the day the issue is simple - digital goods that people paid money for are being taken away from us without any recompense or regard for our feelings as a customer. I believe this would make a good addition to already existing points of this act and would be supported by many people both outside and inside the EU as proven by the success of the "Stop Killing Games initiative" which has, as of now, gathered more than 1.4 million signatures within the EU.
